sections.title: Welcome to Test Page
sections.intro: This is a test documentation page to demonstrate Compass Docs' multi-source capability.

blocks.value: This test page exists to show how Compass Docs can handle multiple documentation sources. You can switch between this page and other documentation using the dropdown selector in the header.
Compass Docs automatically discovers all JSON files in the src/lib/data/json/ directory and makes them available for selection.
This page demonstrates several key features of Compass Docs:
Automatic Discovery: This file was automatically detected and added to the source selector.
Instant Switching: You can switch between documentation sources without page reloads.
Dynamic ToC: The table of contents updates automatically based on the current source.
Progress Tracking: The progress indicator shows your position in this documentation.

sections.title: Content Examples
sections.intro: Here are examples of all the content types supported by Compass Docs.

blocks.value: Text blocks are perfect for paragraphs, descriptions, and explanations. They support automatic URL linking - try visiting https://github.com to see how URLs are automatically converted to clickable links.
You can use bold tags to emphasize important terms or concepts. Text blocks also support basic HTML formatting for rich content.
Code blocks come in three variants: single-line, multi-line, and inline. Here's a single-line example:
yarn install
And here's a multi-line code block showing a function:
Notification blocks highlight important information with visual emphasis. Here are all five types:
Info notes provide helpful context, tips, or additional explanations.
Success notes celebrate achievements or confirm that something worked correctly.
Warning notes alert users to potential issues or things to watch out for.
Error notes highlight critical issues that must be addressed immediately.
Warning-alt provides an alternative warning style with different visual emphasis.

sections.title: Customization Tips
sections.intro: Learn how to customize this test page or create your own documentation pages.

blocks.value: To create a new documentation page like this one, follow these steps:

# 1. Create a new JSON file in src/lib/data/json/
# 2. Follow the Topic → Section → Subtopic → Block structure
# 3. The file will be auto-discovered and added to the selector
# 4. That's it! No additional configuration needed
Compass Docs automatically discovers and registers all JSON files - no manual setup required!
JSON file names are automatically converted to display names in the source selector:
test-documentation.json becomes Test Documentation
api-reference.json becomes Api Reference
user-guide.json becomes User Guide
Use kebab-case (lowercase with hyphens) for file names. They'll be automatically converted to Title Case for display.
Organize your documentation logically using the hierarchical structure:
Sections are major chapters or topics (like chapters in a book)
Subtopics are subsections within each chapter (like subheadings)
Blocks are individual content pieces (paragraphs, code, notes)
Each subtopic should cover one specific concept or task. If it's getting too long, split it into multiple subtopics.

sections.title: Interactive Playground
sections.intro: Use this section to experiment with different content types and see how they render.

blocks.value: You can combine different block types in any order. For example, start with an explanation:
Then add some context or notes about the code:
Always use the latest API version (v1) for new projects. Legacy versions (v0) will be deprecated soon.
And continue with more explanation or examples as needed. The layout automatically adapts to your content structure.
Use descriptive titles: Make subtopic titles clear and specific so users can quickly find what they need.
Break up long text: Use multiple text blocks instead of one huge paragraph. It's easier to read and scan.
Show, don't just tell: Include code examples whenever you're explaining a technical concept.
Use notes strategically: Don't overuse notification blocks - they should highlight truly important information.
You now know how to create and customize documentation with Compass Docs. Start building your own pages!
This subtopic helps test the scroll tracking and table of contents features. As you scroll through the page, notice how:
The current section is highlighted in the table of contents
Sections automatically expand and collapse based on your position
The progress bar updates to show how far you've progressed
Clicking any item in the ToC smoothly scrolls to that section
The IntersectionObserver API tracks your position and updates the UI in real-time for a seamless reading experience.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.intro: This test page demonstrates the core features of Compass Docs. Ready to build your own documentation?

blocks.value: Now that you've seen how Compass Docs works, here's what you can do:
Create your own content: Add JSON files to src/lib/data/json/ with your documentation.
Customize the theme: Use the preferences panel to switch themes, or create your own custom theme.
Deploy your docs: Run yarn build and deploy to GitHub Pages, Netlify, Vercel, or any static host.
Extend functionality: Add new features, components, or block types to suit your needs.
Compass Docs makes documentation beautiful, reactive, and fun to create. Build something amazing!
Check out these resources to learn more:
GitHub Repository: https://github.com/talha-ijaz-qureshi/compass-docs
Carbon Design System: https://carbondesignsystem.com
SvelteKit Documentation: https://kit.svelte.dev
TypeScript Handbook: https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs
